summary: A stellar audience recording that captures a scorching Rooster and a soaring Cassidy, with Let It Grow and other classics delivering an unforgettable 1984 performance.
review: The Walker‑Scotton‑Miller tape is praised as one of the best ’84 audience recordings – full, crisp, and remarkably balanced. Musically the show shines with a fiery Rooster, a stunning Cassidy, and a powerful Let It Grow that many reviewers rank among the era’s finest, despite a few complaints about the ultra‑fast Eyes segment. Overall the performance is high‑energy and tight, making this a standout entry in the fall ’84 run.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (7/8) give 4–5 stars and highlight both superb audio quality and exceptional playing; only one outlier gives a zero rating, so the weighted average leans strongly positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as a beautiful full‑sound Aud with crystal‑clear mix; praised as the best source for this show, complete and without drum dropouts. A few reviewers note occasional off‑rail feel (e.g., Eyes) but overall the tape is crisp and very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set builds into a fiery second set; rare Far From Me appears, followed by classic jams and a soaring Let It Grow climax. Reviewers note strong transitions and sustained momentum throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly called out for his best work of the year – flawless attack, nimble phrasing, and powerful vocals.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as the MVP of the sextet, delivering standout bass performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) praised for playing at a peak level on this tape.
